structure_blocks.heading: Buyer's Guide Manage the solicitation period - Notification of procurement to land claimant groups
heading_path: Buyer's Guide Manage the solicitation period - Notification of procurement to land claimant groups
structure_blocks.text: Notification of procurement to land claimant groups Notifying land claimant group(s) about procurements is a common obligation of the Comprehensive Land Claims Agreements (CLCAs). If you are a Public Service and Procurement Canada (PSPC) buyer, follow the procedures for CLCA s notification outlined in this section. Business directories or lists Under several modern treaties/ CLCA s, the land claimant groups have to prepare and maintain lists of modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary businesses. The business directories/lists identify the types of goods and services that the businesses can supply. For a procurement posted on the Government Electronic Tendering Service (GETS), you should use the business directories of the applicable modern treaties/ CLCA s to notify the modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary firms listed for the applicable commodities, in accordance with Contacting businesses directly during the solicitation period . For a procurement not posted on GETS , you should use the directories to invite modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary businesses to submit an offer. This by itself must not prevent unlisted businesses from participating in the opportunity. For a list of Inuit businesses for the James Bay and Northern Quebec Agreement and for the Nunavik Inuit Land Claims Agreement, consult the Makivvik Business website For a list of Cree businesses for the James Bay and Northern Quebec Agreement, consult the Grand Council of the Crees business directory For a list of Naskapi businesses in the Northeastern Quebec Settlement Area, consult the Naskapi Business Directory For a list of Inuvialuit businesses in the Inuvialuit Settlement Region, consult the Inuvialuit Business List For a list of Gwich'in businesses in the Gwich'in Settlement Area, consult the Gwich'in Business Directory For a list of Inuit businesses in the Nunavut Settlement Area, consult the Inuit Firm Registry For more information on Tlicho businesses, visit the Tlicho Business website For a list of Inuit businesses in the Labrador Inuit Settlement Area, consult the Nunatsiavut Government's Business Directory page For a list of Kwanlin Dun First Nation businesses, consult the Kwanlin Dun Business Listings website For a list of Na-Cho Nyak Dun businesses, consult the Na-Cho Nyak Dun Development Corporation Businesses and Partnerships website For a list of Tsawwassen First Nations businesses, consult the Tsawwassen Business Directory website For a list of Maa-nulth First Nations businesses, consult the following Maa-nulth First Nations business websites Huu-ay-aht First Nations Ka:’yu:’k’t’h’/Che:k’tles7et’h First Nations Toquaht Nation Other business directories Since the above is not an exhaustive list of Modern Treaty/ CLCA land claimant business lists, in certain circumstances, you may consider using other unidentified Modern Treaty/ CLCA beneficiary business directories to ensure that beneficiary businesses are eligible to participate in a federal procurement. Buyers are advised to contact the Indigenous Procurement Policy Directorate or Indigenous Services Canada for assistance. If any links included within this section are found to be non-functional or out of date, please notify the Indigenous Procurement Policy Directorate . Method of notification As a PSPC buyer, you must fax or email a copy of a notice describing the procurement to the land claimant group(s) of each of the CLCA s that apply to the procurement, as detailed in Notification of procurement to Modern Treaty (Comprehensive Land Claims Agreements) claimant groups . Timing and content of notices For procurements to be posted on the GETS , as a PSPC buyer, you must send the notice to the applicable land claimant group(s) on the date of posting, indicating that CLCA s apply. This procedure applies to: Notices of Proposed Procurement (NPPs) Advance Contract Award Notices (ACAN) Letters of Interest (LOI) price and availability (P&A) enquiries For procurements that will not be posted on the GETS , you must send the applicable land claimant group(s) a notice about the procurement, containing the same information that an NPP , ACAN , LOI , or a P&A enquiry would have contained. In such cases, you should allow land claimant group(s) at least 15 calendar days for inquiries before contract award, although CLCA s do not specify a waiting period. Scope of notification As a PSPC buyer, you must notify land claimant group(s) about all procurements, regardless of the procurement instrument used, including: request for quotations (RFQs) requests for proposals (RFPs) requests for standing offers (RFSOs) requests for supply arrangements (RFSAs) solicitations under supply arrangements calls for proposals Individual call-ups against a standing offer do not require separate notification, as notification at the RFSO stage is sufficient.

raw_text:
Notification of procurement to land claimant groups Notifying land claimant group(s) about procurements is a common obligation of the Comprehensive Land Claims Agreements (CLCAs). If you are a Public Service and Procurement Canada (PSPC) buyer, follow the procedures for CLCA s notification outlined in this section. Business directories or lists Under several modern treaties/ CLCA s, the land claimant groups have to prepare and maintain lists of modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary businesses. The business directories/lists identify the types of goods and services that the businesses can supply. For a procurement posted on the Government Electronic Tendering Service (GETS), you should use the business directories of the applicable modern treaties/ CLCA s to notify the modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary firms listed for the applicable commodities, in accordance with Contacting businesses directly during the solicitation period . For a procurement not posted on GETS , you should use the directories to invite modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary businesses to submit an offer. This by itself must not prevent unlisted businesses from participating in the opportunity. For a list of Inuit businesses for the James Bay and Northern Quebec Agreement and for the Nunavik Inuit Land Claims Agreement, consult the Makivvik Business website For a list of Cree businesses for the James Bay and Northern Quebec Agreement, consult the Grand Council of the Crees business directory For a list of Naskapi businesses in the Northeastern Quebec Settlement Area, consult the Naskapi Business Directory For a list of Inuvialuit businesses in the Inuvialuit Settlement Region, consult the Inuvialuit Business List For a list of Gwich'in businesses in the Gwich'in Settlement Area, consult the Gwich'in Business Directory For a list of Inuit businesses in the Nunavut Settlement Area, consult the Inuit Firm Registry For more information on Tlicho businesses, visit the Tlicho Business website For a list of Inuit businesses in the Labrador Inuit Settlement Area, consult the Nunatsiavut Government's Business Directory page For a list of Kwanlin Dun First Nation businesses, consult the Kwanlin Dun Business Listings website For a list of Na-Cho Nyak Dun businesses, consult the Na-Cho Nyak Dun Development Corporation Businesses and Partnerships website For a list of Tsawwassen First Nations businesses, consult the Tsawwassen Business Directory website For a list of Maa-nulth First Nations businesses, consult the following Maa-nulth First Nations business websites Huu-ay-aht First Nations Ka:’yu:’k’t’h’/Che:k’tles7et’h First Nations Toquaht Nation Other business directories Since the above is not an exhaustive list of Modern Treaty/ CLCA land claimant business lists, in certain circumstances, you may consider using other unidentified Modern Treaty/ CLCA beneficiary business directories to ensure that beneficiary businesses are eligible to participate in a federal procurement. Buyers are advised to contact the Indigenous Procurement Policy Directorate or Indigenous Services Canada for assistance. If any links included within this section are found to be non-functional or out of date, please notify the Indigenous Procurement Policy Directorate . Method of notification As a PSPC buyer, you must fax or email a copy of a notice describing the procurement to the land claimant group(s) of each of the CLCA s that apply to the procurement, as detailed in Notification of procurement to Modern Treaty (Comprehensive Land Claims Agreements) claimant groups . Timing and content of notices For procurements to be posted on the GETS , as a PSPC buyer, you must send the notice to the applicable land claimant group(s) on the date of posting, indicating that CLCA s apply. This procedure applies to: Notices of Proposed Procurement (NPPs) Advance Contract Award Notices (ACAN) Letters of Interest (LOI) price and availability (P&A) enquiries For procurements that will not be posted on the GETS , you must send the applicable land claimant group(s) a notice about the procurement, containing the same information that an NPP , ACAN , LOI , or a P&A enquiry would have contained. In such cases, you should allow land claimant group(s) at least 15 calendar days for inquiries before contract award, although CLCA s do not specify a waiting period. Scope of notification As a PSPC buyer, you must notify land claimant group(s) about all procurements, regardless of the procurement instrument used, including: request for quotations (RFQs) requests for proposals (RFPs) requests for standing offers (RFSOs) requests for supply arrangements (RFSAs) solicitations under supply arrangements calls for proposals Individual call-ups against a standing offer do not require separate notification, as notification at the RFSO stage is sufficient.

Notification of procurement to land claimant groups

Notifying land claimant group(s) about procurements is a common obligation of the Comprehensive Land Claims Agreements (CLCAs). If you are a Public Service and Procurement Canada (PSPC) buyer, follow the procedures for CLCA s notification outlined in this section.

Business directories or lists

Under several modern treaties/ CLCA s, the land claimant groups have to prepare and maintain lists of modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary businesses. The business directories/lists identify the types of goods and services that the businesses can supply.

For a procurement posted on the Government Electronic Tendering Service (GETS), you should use the business directories of the applicable modern treaties/ CLCA s to notify the modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary firms listed for the applicable commodities, in accordance with Contacting businesses directly during the solicitation period .

For a procurement not posted on GETS , you should use the directories to invite modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary businesses to submit an offer. This by itself must not prevent unlisted businesses from participating in the opportunity.

For a list of Inuit businesses for the James Bay and Northern Quebec Agreement and for the Nunavik Inuit Land Claims Agreement, consult the Makivvik Business website

For a list of Cree businesses for the James Bay and Northern Quebec Agreement, consult the Grand Council of the Crees business directory

For a list of Naskapi businesses in the Northeastern Quebec Settlement Area, consult the Naskapi Business Directory

For a list of Inuvialuit businesses in the Inuvialuit Settlement Region, consult the Inuvialuit Business List

For a list of Gwich'in businesses in the Gwich'in Settlement Area, consult the Gwich'in Business Directory

For a list of Inuit businesses in the Nunavut Settlement Area, consult the Inuit Firm Registry

For more information on Tlicho businesses, visit the Tlicho Business website

For a list of Inuit businesses in the Labrador Inuit Settlement Area, consult the Nunatsiavut Government's Business Directory page

For a list of Kwanlin Dun First Nation businesses, consult the Kwanlin Dun Business Listings website

For a list of Na-Cho Nyak Dun businesses, consult the Na-Cho Nyak Dun Development Corporation Businesses and Partnerships website

For a list of Tsawwassen First Nations businesses, consult the Tsawwassen Business Directory website

For a list of Maa-nulth First Nations businesses, consult the following Maa-nulth First Nations business websites Huu-ay-aht First Nations Ka:’yu:’k’t’h’/Che:k’tles7et’h First Nations Toquaht Nation

Huu-ay-aht First Nations

Ka:’yu:’k’t’h’/Che:k’tles7et’h First Nations

Toquaht Nation

Other business directories

Since the above is not an exhaustive list of Modern Treaty/ CLCA land claimant business lists, in certain circumstances, you may consider using other unidentified Modern Treaty/ CLCA beneficiary business directories to ensure that beneficiary businesses are eligible to participate in a federal procurement. Buyers are advised to contact the Indigenous Procurement Policy Directorate or Indigenous Services Canada for assistance.

If any links included within this section are found to be non-functional or out of date, please notify the Indigenous Procurement Policy Directorate .

Method of notification

As a PSPC buyer, you must fax or email a copy of a notice describing the procurement to the land claimant group(s) of each of the CLCA s that apply to the procurement, as detailed in Notification of procurement to Modern Treaty (Comprehensive Land Claims Agreements) claimant groups .

Timing and content of notices

For procurements to be posted on the GETS , as a PSPC buyer, you must send the notice to the applicable land claimant group(s) on the date of posting, indicating that CLCA s apply. This procedure applies to:

Notices of Proposed Procurement (NPPs)

Advance Contract Award Notices (ACAN)

Letters of Interest (LOI)

price and availability (P&A) enquiries

For procurements that will not be posted on the GETS , you must send the applicable land claimant group(s) a notice about the procurement, containing the same information that an NPP , ACAN , LOI , or a P&A enquiry would have contained. In such cases, you should allow land claimant group(s) at least 15 calendar days for inquiries before contract award, although CLCA s do not specify a waiting period.

Scope of notification

As a PSPC buyer, you must notify land claimant group(s) about all procurements, regardless of the procurement instrument used, including:

request for quotations (RFQs)

requests for proposals (RFPs)

requests for standing offers (RFSOs)

requests for supply arrangements (RFSAs)

solicitations under supply arrangements

calls for proposals

Individual call-ups against a standing offer do not require separate notification, as notification at the RFSO stage is sufficient.

For a procurement posted on the Government Electronic Tendering Service (GETS), you should use the business directories of the applicable modern treaties/ CLCA s to notify the modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary firms listed for the applicable commodities, in accordance with Contacting businesses directly during the solicitation period .

For a procurement not posted on GETS , you should use the directories to invite modern treaty/ CLCA beneficiary businesses to submit an offer. This by itself must not prevent unlisted businesses from participating in the opportunity.

For a list of Inuit businesses for the James Bay and Northern Quebec Agreement and for the Nunavik Inuit Land Claims Agreement, consult the Makivvik Business website

For a list of Cree businesses for the James Bay and Northern Quebec Agreement, consult the Grand Council of the Crees business directory

For a list of Naskapi businesses in the Northeastern Quebec Settlement Area, consult the Naskapi Business Directory

For a list of Inuvialuit businesses in the Inuvialuit Settlement Region, consult the Inuvialuit Business List

For a list of Gwich'in businesses in the Gwich'in Settlement Area, consult the Gwich'in Business Directory

For a list of Inuit businesses in the Nunavut Settlement Area, consult the Inuit Firm Registry

For more information on Tlicho businesses, visit the Tlicho Business website

For a list of Inuit businesses in the Labrador Inuit Settlement Area, consult the Nunatsiavut Government's Business Directory page

For a list of Kwanlin Dun First Nation businesses, consult the Kwanlin Dun Business Listings website

For a list of Na-Cho Nyak Dun businesses, consult the Na-Cho Nyak Dun Development Corporation Businesses and Partnerships website

For a list of Tsawwassen First Nations businesses, consult the Tsawwassen Business Directory website

For a list of Maa-nulth First Nations businesses, consult the following Maa-nulth First Nations business websites Huu-ay-aht First Nations Ka:’yu:’k’t’h’/Che:k’tles7et’h First Nations Toquaht Nation

Huu-ay-aht First Nations

Ka:’yu:’k’t’h’/Che:k’tles7et’h First Nations

Toquaht Nation

Other business directories

Since the above is not an exhaustive list of Modern Treaty/ CLCA land claimant business lists, in certain circumstances, you may consider using other unidentified Modern Treaty/ CLCA beneficiary business directories to ensure that beneficiary businesses are eligible to participate in a federal procurement. Buyers are advised to contact the Indigenous Procurement Policy Directorate or Indigenous Services Canada for assistance.

If any links included within this section are found to be non-functional or out of date, please notify the Indigenous Procurement Policy Directorate .

Method of notification

As a PSPC buyer, you must fax or email a copy of a notice describing the procurement to the land claimant group(s) of each of the CLCA s that apply to the procurement, as detailed in Notification of procurement to Modern Treaty (Comprehensive Land Claims Agreements) claimant groups .

Timing and content of notices

For procurements to be posted on the GETS , as a PSPC buyer, you must send the notice to the applicable land claimant group(s) on the date of posting, indicating that CLCA s apply. This procedure applies to:

Notices of Proposed Procurement (NPPs)

Advance Contract Award Notices (ACAN)

Letters of Interest (LOI)

price and availability (P&A) enquiries

For procurements that will not be posted on the GETS , you must send the applicable land claimant group(s) a notice about the procurement, containing the same information that an NPP , ACAN , LOI , or a P&A enquiry would have contained. In such cases, you should allow land claimant group(s) at least 15 calendar days for inquiries before contract award, although CLCA s do not specify a waiting period.

Scope of notification

As a PSPC buyer, you must notify land claimant group(s) about all procurements, regardless of the procurement instrument used, including:

request for quotations (RFQs)

requests for proposals (RFPs)

requests for standing offers (RFSOs)

requests for supply arrangements (RFSAs)

solicitations under supply arrangements

calls for proposals

Individual call-ups against a standing offer do not require separate notification, as notification at the RFSO stage is sufficient.
